translation: And Josiah begot Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the deportation to Babylon.

alternate_translations.translation: Josiah then fathered Jeconiah and his brothers during the Babylonian exile.
alternate_translations.notes: “ἐπὶ τῆς μετοικεσίας Βαβυλῶνος” can be rendered as either “at the time of/during” the deportation, or more idiomatically “during the exile.” Both capture the same sense.

alternate_translations.translation: Now Josiah begot Jeconiah and his brothers when Babylon carried them away.
alternate_translations.notes: A more interpretive phrasing: “μετοικεσία” denotes a forced transfer/deportation, so “carried them away” emphasizes coercion.

notes: Greek construction: “Ἰωσίας δὲ” (“and/but … then”) is a common narrative connective. The key phrase “ἐπὶ τῆς μετοικεσίας Βαβυλῶνος” indicates the time reference—Jeconiah and his brothers are said to be born “during/at the time of” the Babylonian deportation. “μετοικεσία” specifically points to deportation/relocation rather than a voluntary migration.
